articles.title: Oil exploration only after resolving Assam-Nagaland border disputes: Konyak Union
articles.sum: The apex body of the Konyak Nagas says territorial clarity, landowners’ consent, and stronger border security must precede implementation of the bilateral agreement

articles.title: Centre bans paraquat dichloride, a herbicide linked to thousands of self-harm, homicide and accidental deaths
articles.sum: India joins the long list of over 70 countries that have banned the toxic herbicide which has no antidote

articles.title: Amended RPwD Act to include persons internally injured by acid ingestion, Centre tells Supreme Court
articles.sum: The 2016 law had previously recognised only victims of acid-throwing and not forcible acid ingestion; the amendment will have a retrospective effect

articles.title: Kerala HC directs Defence Ministry to file report on hazardous cargo threat from MSC Elsa 3 shipwreck
articles.sum: Assessing the survey reports, the court noted that approximately 84 containers were partially embedded in the seabed, and several had structural damage

articles.title: Project to fill 88 tanks in Haveri district will be helpful in rain-deficit years, says Karnataka’s PWD Minister Satish Jarkiholi
articles.sum: PWD Minister Satish Jarkiholi says the government of Karnataka has chalked out a plan to construct check dams on Tunga and Varada rivers in the district, which would help in improving the ground water table

articles.title: Land acquisition from tribal council area for Guwahati expansion project opposed
articles.sum: The local units of the Bodo and Rabha students’ bodies say the proposed acquisition would violate the provisions of the Rabha Hasong Autonomous Council Act and adversely affect tribal land rights

articles.title: 'Received nothing but silence': CJP's Dipke slams Centre as Sonam Wangchuk's fast enters Day 18
articles.sum: Abhijeet Dipke criticized the government's silence regarding Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike. Wangchuk has been fasting for eighteen days protesting examination irregularities and paper leaks. He questioned why the Prime Minister has not engaged in dialogue. Opposition leaders hav...

articles.title: 'If she publicly says ...': Congress extends Martyrs' Day invite to Mamata, but with a condition
articles.sum: The Congress invited Mamata Banerjee to its Martyrs' Day event in Kolkata. This invitation came with a condition that she acknowledge leaving Congress was a mistake. West Bengal Congress president stated Mamata should not distort the historical significance of the 1993 movement. ...

articles.title: ADAG companies siphoned off loan money via 53 shell firms: ED
articles.sum: Enforcement Directorate informed the Supreme Court about Reliance ADAG entities siphoning funds. These companies allegedly used fifty-three shell entities to divert bank loans. Assets worth over Rs 5,500 crore have been attached by the agency. Other Reliance group companies also ...

articles.title: Supreme Court acquits man, 16 others in 26-year-old dowry case
articles.sum: The Supreme Court acquitted seventeen individuals accused in a dowry death case. This decision came after a lengthy twenty-six-year legal battle for the family. The court criticized lower courts for disregarding defense evidence without valid reasons. Substantive testimonies and ...

articles.title: NCPI plans MPs meeting as Birla likely to decide on its recognition plea before session
articles.sum: Rebel TMC MPs are set to hold their parliamentary party meeting under NCPI. They anticipate representation at the customary pre-session all-party meeting. Speaker Om Birla will decide on merger applications before the monsoon session begins. Recognition of NCPI could make it BJP'...

articles.title: Supreme Court fines Raina, 4 others Rs 3 lakh each for taking court for ride
articles.sum: The Supreme Court imposed a three lakh rupee cost on Samay Raina and four others. These comedians failed to hold a promised show for disabled individuals. They had previously faced court anger for mocking people with disabilities. The court accused Raina of lying and taking the j...

articles.title: SOP soon to hear petitions after working hours, says Supreme Court
articles.sum: The Supreme Court will create a procedure for urgent petitions. This new system will address life and liberty concerns after court hours. The court agreed to explore this after a lawyer's plea for immediate access. Only cases involving life and liberty infringements will be consi...

articles.title: From Wimbledon towels to Scotch: What India-UK trade deal could mean for shoppers
articles.sum: The free trade agreement between the world's fifth and sixth largest economies has come into effect - can it deliver?

articles.title: How India's Lord's win echoes the legacy of its pioneering women cricketers
articles.sum: India won the first women's Test at Lord's, 142 years after its first men's Test.

articles.title: PM Modi helped stop Putin from using nukes in Ukraine, claims Polish minister
articles.sum: PM Modi helped stop Putin from using nukes in Ukraine claims Polish minister
